translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There was a man in the land of Uz, whose name was Job. That man was blameless and upright, and one who feared God,  and turned away from evil.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There were born to him seven sons and three daughters.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: His possessions also were seven thousand sheep, three thousand camels, five hundred yoke of oxen, five hundred female donkeys, and a very great household; so that this man was the greatest of all the children of the east.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: His sons went and held a feast in the house of each one on his birthday; and they sent and called for their three sisters to eat and to drink with them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It was so, when the days of their feasting had run their course, that Job sent and sanctified them, and rose up early in the morning, and offered burnt offerings according to the number of them all. For Job said, “It may be that my sons have sinned, and renounced God in their hearts.” Job did so continually.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now on the day when God’s sons came to present themselves before Yahweh,  Satan also came among them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh said to Satan, “Where have you come from?” Then Satan answered Yahweh, and said, “From going back and forth in the earth, and from walking up and down in it.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh said to Satan, “Have you considered my servant, Job? For there is no one like him in the earth, a blameless and an upright man, one who fears God, and turns away from evil.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Satan answered Yahweh, and said, “Does Job fear God for nothing?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Haven’t you made a hedge around him, and around his house, and around all that he has, on every side? You have blessed the work of his hands, and his substance is increased in the land.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But stretch out your hand now, and touch all that he has, and he will renounce you to your face.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh said to Satan, “Behold,  all that he has is in your power. Only on himself don’t stretch out your hand.” So Satan went out from the presence of Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It fell on a day when his sons and his daughters were eating and drinking wine in their oldest brother’s house,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: that there came a messenger to Job, and said, “The oxen were plowing, and the donkeys feeding beside them,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and the Sabeans attacked, and took them away. Yes, they have killed the servants with the edge of the sword, and I alone have escaped to tell you.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: While he was still speaking, there also came another, and said, “The fire of God has fallen from the sky, and has burned up the sheep and the servants, and consumed them, and I alone have escaped to tell you.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: While he was still speaking, there came also another, and said, “The Chaldeans made three bands, and swept down on the camels, and have taken them away, yes, and killed the servants with the edge of the sword; and I alone have escaped to tell you.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: While he was still speaking, there came also another, and said, “Your sons and your daughters were eating and drinking wine in their oldest brother’s house,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and behold, there came a great wind from the wilderness, and struck the four corners of the house, and it fell on the young men, and they are dead. I alone have escaped to tell you.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Job arose, and tore his robe, and shaved his head, and fell down on the ground, and worshiped.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said, “Naked I came out of my mother’s womb, and naked shall I return there. Yahweh gave, and Yahweh has taken away. Blessed be Yahweh’s name.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In all this, Job did not sin, nor charge God with wrongdoing.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Again, on the day when the God’s sons came to present themselves before Yahweh, Satan came also among them to present himself before Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh said to Satan, “Where have you come from?” Satan answered Yahweh, and said, “From going back and forth in the earth, and from walking up and down in it.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh said to Satan, “Have you considered my servant Job? For there is no one like him in the earth, a blameless and an upright man, one who fears God, and turns away from evil. He still maintains his integrity, although you incited me against him, to ruin him without cause.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Satan answered Yahweh, and said, “Skin for skin. Yes, all that a man has he will give for his life.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But stretch out your hand now, and touch his bone and his flesh, and he will renounce you to your face.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh said to Satan, “Behold, he is in your hand. Only spare his life.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So Satan went out from the presence of Yahweh, and struck Job with painful sores from the sole of his foot to his head.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He took for himself a potsherd to scrape himself with, and he sat among the ashes.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then his wife said to him, “Do you still maintain your integrity? Renounce God, and die.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But he said to her, “You speak as one of the foolish women would speak. What? Shall we receive good at the hand of God, and shall we not receive evil?” In all this Job didn’t sin with his lips.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now when Job’s three friends heard of all this evil that had come on him, they each came from his own place: Eliphaz the Temanite, Bildad the Shuhite, and Zophar the Naamathite; and they made an appointment together to come to sympathize with him and to comfort him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When they lifted up their eyes from a distance, and didn’t recognize him, they raised their voices, and wept; and they each tore his robe, and sprinkled dust on their heads toward the sky.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So they sat down with him on the ground seven days and seven nights, and no one spoke a word to him, for they saw that his grief was very great.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: After this Job opened his mouth, and cursed the day of his birth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Job answered:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Let the day perish in which I was born, the night which said, ‘There is a boy conceived.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Let that day be darkness. Don’t let God from above seek for it, neither let the light shine on it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Let darkness and the shadow of death claim it for their own. Let a cloud dwell on it. Let all that makes black the day terrify it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As for that night, let thick darkness seize on it. Let it not rejoice among the days of the year. Let it not come into the number of the months.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, let that night be barren. Let no joyful voice come therein.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Let them curse it who curse the day, who are ready to rouse up leviathan.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Let the stars of its twilight be dark. Let it look for light, but have none, neither let it see the eyelids of the morning,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: because it didn’t shut up the doors of my mother’s womb, nor did it hide trouble from my eyes.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Why didn’t I die from the womb? Why didn’t I give up the spirit when my mother bore me?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Why did the knees receive me? Or why the breast, that I should nurse?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For now should I have lain down and been quiet. I should have slept, then I would have been at rest,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: with kings and counselors of the earth, who built up waste places for themselves;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: or with princes who had gold, who filled their houses with silver:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: or as a hidden untimely birth I had not been, as infants who never saw light.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There the wicked cease from troubling. There the weary are at rest.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There the prisoners are at ease together. They don’t hear the voice of the taskmaster.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The small and the great are there. The servant is free from his master.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Why is light given to him who is in misery, life to the bitter in soul,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Who long for death, but it doesn’t come; and dig for it more than for hidden treasures,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: who rejoice exceedingly, and are glad, when they can find the grave?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Why is light given to a man whose way is hidden, whom God has hedged in?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For my sighing comes before I eat. My groanings are poured out like water.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the thing which I fear comes on me, That which I am afraid of comes to me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I am not at ease, neither am I quiet, neither have I rest; but trouble comes.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “If someone ventures to talk with you, will you be grieved? But who can withhold himself from speaking?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, you have instructed many, you have strengthened the weak hands.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Your words have supported him who was falling, You have made firm the feeble knees.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But now it has come to you, and you faint. It touches you, and you are troubled.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Isn’t your piety your confidence? Isn’t the integrity of your ways your hope?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Remember, now, whoever perished, being innocent? Or where were the upright cut off?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: According to what I have seen, those who plow iniquity, and sow trouble, reap the same.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: By the breath of God they perish. By the blast of his anger are they consumed.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The roaring of the lion, and the voice of the fierce lion, the teeth of the young lions, are broken.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The old lion perishes for lack of prey. The cubs of the lioness are scattered abroad.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Now a thing was secretly brought to me. My ear received a whisper of it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In thoughts from the visions of the night, when deep sleep falls on men,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: fear came on me, and trembling, which made all my bones shake.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then a spirit passed before my face. The hair of my flesh stood up.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It stood still, but I couldn’t discern its appearance. A form was before my eyes. Silence, then I heard a voice, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: ‘Shall mortal man be more just than God? Shall a man be more pure than his Maker?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, he puts no trust in his servants. He charges his angels with error.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: How much more, those who dwell in houses of clay, whose foundation is in the dust, who are crushed before the moth!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Between morning and evening they are destroyed. They perish forever without any regarding it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Isn’t their tent cord plucked up within them? They die, and that without wisdom.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Call now; is there any who will answer you? To which of the holy ones will you turn?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For resentment kills the foolish man, and jealousy kills the simple.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I have seen the foolish taking root, but suddenly I cursed his habitation.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: His children are far from safety. They are crushed in the gate. Neither is there any to deliver them,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: whose harvest the hungry eats up, and take it even out of the thorns. The snare gapes for their substance.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For affliction doesn’t come out of the dust, neither does trouble spring out of the ground;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: but man is born to trouble, as the sparks fly upward.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “But as for me, I would seek God. I would commit my cause to God,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: who does great things that can’t be fathomed, marvelous things without number;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: who gives rain on the earth, and sends waters on the fields;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: so that he sets up on high those who are low, those who mourn are exalted to safety.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He frustrates the devices of the crafty, So that their hands can’t perform their enterprise.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He takes the wise in their own craftiness; the counsel of the cunning is carried headlong.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They meet with darkness in the day time, and grope at noonday as in the night.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But he saves from the sword of their mouth, even the needy from the hand of the mighty.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So the poor has hope, and injustice shuts her mouth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Behold, happy is the man whom God corrects. Therefore do not despise the chastening of the Almighty.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For he wounds, and binds up. He injures, and his hands make whole.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He will deliver you in six troubles; yes, in seven no evil shall touch you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In famine he will redeem you from death; in war, from the power of the sword.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall be hidden from the scourge of the tongue, neither shall you be afraid of destruction when it comes.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: At destruction and famine you shall laugh, neither shall you be afraid of the animals of the earth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For you shall be allied with the stones of the field. The animals of the field shall be at peace with you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall know that your tent is in peace. You shall visit your fold, and shall miss nothing.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall know also that your offspring  shall be great, Your offspring as the grass of the earth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall come to your grave in a full age, like a shock of grain comes in its season.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Look this, we have searched it, so it is. Hear it, and know it for your good.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Job answered,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Oh that my anguish were weighed, and all my calamity laid in the balances!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For now it would be heavier than the sand of the seas, therefore have my words been rash.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the arrows of the Almighty are within me. My spirit drinks up their poison. The terrors of God set themselves in array against me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Does the wild donkey bray when he has grass? Or does the ox low over his fodder?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Can that which has no flavor be eaten without salt? Or is there any taste in the white of an egg?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My soul refuses to touch them. They are as loathsome food to me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Oh that I might have my request, that God would grant the thing that I long for,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: even that it would please God to crush me; that he would let loose his hand, and cut me off!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Be it still my consolation, yes, let me exult in pain that doesn’t spare, that I have not denied the words of the Holy One.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: What is my strength, that I should wait? What is my end, that I should be patient?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Is my strength the strength of stones? Or is my flesh of brass?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Isn’t it that I have no help in me, That wisdom is driven quite from me?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “To him who is ready to faint, kindness should be shown from his friend; even to him who forsakes the fear of the Almighty.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My brothers have dealt deceitfully as a brook, as the channel of brooks that pass away;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Which are black by reason of the ice, in which the snow hides itself.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In the dry season, they vanish. When it is hot, they are consumed out of their place.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The caravans that travel beside them turn aside. They go up into the waste, and perish.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The caravans of Tema looked. The companies of Sheba waited for them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They were distressed because they were confident. They came there, and were confounded.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For now you are nothing. You see a terror, and are afraid.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Did I say, ‘Give to me?’ or, ‘Offer a present for me from your substance?’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: or, ‘Deliver me from the adversary’s hand?’ or, ‘Redeem me from the hand of the oppressors?’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Teach me, and I will hold my peace. Cause me to understand wherein I have erred.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: How forcible are words of uprightness! But your reproof, what does it reprove?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Do you intend to reprove words, since the speeches of one who is desperate are as wind?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yes, you would even cast lots for the fatherless, and make merchandise of your friend.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now therefore be pleased to look at me, for surely I shall not lie to your face.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Please return. Let there be no injustice. Yes, return again. My cause is righteous.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Is there injustice on my tongue? Can’t my taste discern mischievous things?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Isn’t a man forced to labor on earth? Aren’t his days like the days of a hired hand?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As a servant who earnestly desires the shadow, as a hireling who looks for his wages,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: so am I made to possess months of misery, wearisome nights are appointed to me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When I lie down, I say, ‘When shall I arise, and the night be gone?’ I toss and turn until the dawning of the day.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My flesh is clothed with worms and clods of dust. My skin closes up, and breaks out afresh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My days are swifter than a weaver’s shuttle, and are spent without hope.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Oh remember that my life is a breath. My eye shall no more see good.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The eye of him who sees me shall see me no more. Your eyes shall be on me, but I shall not be.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As the cloud is consumed and vanishes away, so he who goes down to Sheol  shall come up no more.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He shall return no more to his house, neither shall his place know him any more.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Therefore I will not keep silent. I will speak in the anguish of my spirit. I will complain in the bitterness of my soul.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Am I a sea, or a sea monster, that you put a guard over me?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When I say, ‘My bed shall comfort me. My couch shall ease my complaint;’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: then you scare me with dreams, and terrify me through visions:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: so that my soul chooses strangling, death rather than my bones.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I loathe my life. I don’t want to live forever. Leave me alone, for my days are but a breath.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: What is man, that you should magnify him, that you should set your mind on him,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: that you should visit him every morning, and test him every moment?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: How long will you not look away from me, nor leave me alone until I swallow down my spittle?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If I have sinned, what do I do to you, you watcher of men? Why have you set me as a mark for you, so that I am a burden to myself?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Why do you not pardon my disobedience, and take away my iniquity? For now shall I lie down in the dust. You will seek me diligently, but I shall not be.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Bildad the Shuhite answered,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “How long will you speak these things? Shall the words of your mouth be a mighty wind?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Does God pervert justice? Or does the Almighty pervert righteousness?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If your children have sinned against him, He has delivered them into the hand of their disobedience.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If you want to seek God diligently, make your supplication to the Almighty.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If you were pure and upright, surely now he would awaken for you, and make the habitation of your righteousness prosperous.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Though your beginning was small, yet your latter end would greatly increase.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Please inquire of past generations. Find out about the learning of their fathers.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: (For we are but of yesterday, and know nothing, because our days on earth are a shadow.)

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Shall they not teach you, tell you, and utter words out of their heart?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Can the papyrus grow up without mire? Can the rushes grow without water?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: While it is yet in its greenness, not cut down, it withers before any other reed.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So are the paths of all who forget God. The hope of the godless man shall perish,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Whose confidence shall break apart, Whose trust is a spider’s web.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He shall lean on his house, but it shall not stand. He shall cling to it, but it shall not endure.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He is green before the sun. His shoots go out along his garden.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: His roots are wrapped around the rock pile. He sees the place of stones.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If he is destroyed from his place, then it shall deny him, saying, ‘I have not seen you.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, this is the joy of his way: out of the earth, others shall spring.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Behold, God will not cast away a blameless man, neither will he uphold the evildoers.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He will still fill your mouth with laughter, your lips with shouting.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Those who hate you shall be clothed with shame. The tent of the wicked shall be no more.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Job answered,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Truly I know that it is so, but how can man be just with God?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If he is pleased to contend with him, he can’t answer him one time in a thousand.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: God who is wise in heart, and mighty in strength: who has hardened himself against him, and prospered?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He removes the mountains, and they don’t know it, when he overturns them in his anger.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He shakes the earth out of its place. Its pillars tremble.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He commands the sun, and it doesn’t rise, and seals up the stars.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He alone stretches out the heavens, and treads on the waves of the sea.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He makes the Bear, Orion, and the Pleiades, and the rooms of the south.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He does great things past finding out; yes, marvelous things without number.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, he goes by me, and I don’t see him. He passes on also, but I don’t perceive him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, he snatches away. Who can hinder him? Who will ask him, ‘What are you doing?’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “God will not withdraw his anger. The helpers of Rahab stoop under him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: How much less shall I answer him, And choose my words to argue with him?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Though I were righteous, yet I wouldn’t answer him. I would make supplication to my judge.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If I had called, and he had answered me, yet I wouldn’t believe that he listened to my voice.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For he breaks me with a storm, and multiplies my wounds without cause.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He will not allow me to catch my breath, but fills me with bitterness.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If it is a matter of strength, behold, he is mighty! If of justice, ‘Who,’ says he, ‘will summon me?’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Though I am righteous, my own mouth shall condemn me. Though I am blameless, it shall prove me perverse.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I am blameless. I don’t respect myself. I despise my life.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “It is all the same. Therefore I say he destroys the blameless and the wicked.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If the scourge kills suddenly, he will mock at the trial of the innocent.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The earth is given into the hand of the wicked. He covers the faces of its judges. If not he, then who is it?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Now my days are swifter than a runner. They flee away, they see no good.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They have passed away as the swift ships, as the eagle that swoops on the prey.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If I say, ‘I will forget my complaint, I will put off my sad face, and cheer up;’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I am afraid of all my sorrows, I know that you will not hold me innocent.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I shall be condemned. Why then do I labor in vain?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If I wash myself with snow, and cleanse my hands with lye,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: yet you will plunge me in the ditch. My own clothes shall abhor me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For he is not a man, as I am, that I should answer him, that we should come together in judgment.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There is no umpire between us, that might lay his hand on us both.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Let him take his rod away from me. Let his terror not make me afraid;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: then I would speak, and not fear him, for I am not so in myself.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “My soul is weary of my life. I will give free course to my complaint. I will speak in the bitterness of my soul.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will tell God, ‘Do not condemn me. Show me why you contend with me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Is it good to you that you should oppress, that you should despise the work of your hands, and smile on the counsel of the wicked?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Do you have eyes of flesh? Or do you see as man sees?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Are your days as the days of mortals, or your years as man’s years,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: that you inquire after my iniquity, and search after my sin?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Although you know that I am not wicked, there is no one who can deliver out of your hand.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “‘Your hands have framed me and fashioned me altogether, yet you destroy me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Remember, I beg you, that you have fashioned me as clay. Will you bring me into dust again?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Haven’t you poured me out like milk, and curdled me like cheese?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You have clothed me with skin and flesh, and knit me together with bones and sinews.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You have granted me life and loving kindness. Your visitation has preserved my spirit.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yet you hid these things in your heart. I know that this is with you:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: if I sin, then you mark me. You will not acquit me from my iniquity.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If I am wicked, woe to me. If I am righteous, I still shall not lift up my head, being filled with disgrace, and conscious of my affliction.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If my head is held high, you hunt me like a lion. Again you show yourself powerful to me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You renew your witnesses against me, and increase your indignation on me. Changes and warfare are with me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “‘Why, then, have you brought me out of the womb? I wish I had given up the spirit, and no eye had seen me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I should have been as though I had not been. I should have been carried from the womb to the grave.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Aren’t my days few? Cease then. Leave me alone, that I may find a little comfort,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: before I go where I shall not return from, to the land of darkness and of the shadow of death;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: the land dark as midnight, of the shadow of death, without any order, where the light is as midnight.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Zophar, the Naamathite, answered,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Shouldn’t the multitude of words be answered? Should a man full of talk be justified?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Should your boastings make men hold their peace? When you mock, shall no man make you ashamed?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For you say, ‘My doctrine is pure. I am clean in your eyes.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But oh that God would speak, and open his lips against you,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: that he would show you the secrets of wisdom! For true wisdom has two sides. Know therefore that God exacts of you less than your iniquity deserves.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Can you fathom the mystery of God? Or can you probe the limits of the Almighty?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They are high as heaven. What can you do? They are deeper than Sheol.  What can you know?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Its measure is longer than the earth, and broader than the sea.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If he passes by, or confines, or convenes a court, then who can oppose him?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For he knows false men. He sees iniquity also, even though he doesn’t consider it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: An empty-headed man becomes wise when a man is born as a wild donkey’s colt.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “If you set your heart aright, stretch out your hands toward him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If iniquity is in your hand, put it far away. Don’t let unrighteousness dwell in your tents.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Surely then you shall lift up your face without spot; Yes, you shall be steadfast, and shall not fear:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: for you shall forget your misery. You shall remember it like waters that have passed away.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Life shall be clearer than the noonday. Though there is darkness, it shall be as the morning.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall be secure, because there is hope. Yes, you shall search, and shall take your rest in safety.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Also you shall lie down, and no one shall make you afraid. Yes, many shall court your favor.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But the eyes of the wicked shall fail. They shall have no way to flee. Their hope shall be the giving up of the spirit.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Job answered,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “No doubt, but you are the people, and wisdom shall die with you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But I have understanding as well as you; I am not inferior to you. Yes, who doesn’t know such things as these?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I am like one who is a joke to his neighbor, I, who called on God, and he answered. The just, the blameless man is a joke.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In the thought of him who is at ease there is contempt for misfortune. It is ready for them whose foot slips.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The tents of robbers prosper. Those who provoke God are secure, who carry their God in their hands.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “But ask the animals, now, and they shall teach you; the birds of the sky, and they shall tell you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Or speak to the earth, and it shall teach you. The fish of the sea shall declare to you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Who doesn’t know that in all these, Yahweh’s hand has done this,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: in whose hand is the life of every living thing, and the breath of all mankind?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Doesn’t the ear try words, even as the palate tastes its food?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: With aged men is wisdom, in length of days understanding.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “With God is wisdom and might. He has counsel and understanding.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, he breaks down, and it can’t be built again. He imprisons a man, and there can be no release.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, he withholds the waters, and they dry up. Again, he sends them out, and they overturn the earth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: With him is strength and wisdom. The deceived and the deceiver are his.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He leads counselors away stripped. He makes judges fools.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He loosens the bond of kings. He binds their waist with a belt.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He leads priests away stripped, and overthrows the mighty.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He removes the speech of those who are trusted, and takes away the understanding of the elders.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He pours contempt on princes, and loosens the belt of the strong.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He uncovers deep things out of darkness, and brings out to light the shadow of death.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He increases the nations, and he destroys them. He enlarges the nations, and he leads them captive.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He takes away understanding from the chiefs of the people of the earth, and causes them to wander in a wilderness where there is no way.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They grope in the dark without light. He makes them stagger like a drunken man.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Behold, my eye has seen all this. My ear has heard and understood it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: What you know, I know also. I am not inferior to you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Surely I would speak to the Almighty. I desire to reason with God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But you are forgers of lies. You are all physicians of no value.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Oh that you would be completely silent! Then you would be wise.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Hear now my reasoning. Listen to the pleadings of my lips.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Will you speak unrighteously for God, and talk deceitfully for him?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Will you show partiality to him? Will you contend for God?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Is it good that he should search you out? Or as one deceives a man, will you deceive him?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He will surely reprove you, if you secretly show partiality.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Shall not his majesty make you afraid, And his dread fall on you?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Your memorable sayings are proverbs of ashes, Your defenses are defenses of clay.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Be silent, leave me alone, that I may speak. Let come on me what will.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Why should I take my flesh in my teeth, and put my life in my hand?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, he will kill me. I have no hope. Nevertheless, I will maintain my ways before him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: This also shall be my salvation, that a godless man shall not come before him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Hear diligently my speech. Let my declaration be in your ears.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: See now, I have set my cause in order. I know that I am righteous.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Who is he who will contend with me? For then would I hold my peace and give up the spirit.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Only don’t do two things to me; then I will not hide myself from your face:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: withdraw your hand far from me; and don’t let your terror make me afraid.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then call, and I will answer; or let me speak, and you answer me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: How many are my iniquities and sins? Make me know my disobedience and my sin.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Why hide you your face, and hold me for your enemy?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Will you harass a driven leaf? Will you pursue the dry stubble?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For you write bitter things against me, and make me inherit the iniquities of my youth:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You also put my feet in the stocks, and mark all my paths. You set a bound to the soles of my feet,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: though I am decaying like a rotten thing, like a garment that is moth-eaten.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Man, who is born of a woman, is of few days, and full of trouble.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He grows up like a flower, and is cut down. He also flees like a shadow, and doesn’t continue.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Do you open your eyes on such a one, and bring me into judgment with you?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Who can bring a clean thing out of an unclean? Not one.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Seeing his days are determined, the number of his months is with you, and you have appointed his bounds that he can’t pass;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Look away from him, that he may rest, until he shall accomplish, as a hireling, his day.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “For there is hope for a tree, If it is cut down, that it will sprout again, that the tender branch of it will not cease.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Though its root grows old in the earth, and its stock dies in the ground,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: yet through the scent of water it will bud, and sprout boughs like a plant.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But man dies, and is laid low. Yes, man gives up the spirit, and where is he?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As the waters fail from the sea, and the river wastes and dries up,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: so man lies down and doesn’t rise. Until the heavens are no more, they shall not awake, nor be roused out of their sleep.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Oh that you would hide me in Sheol,  that you would keep me secret, until your wrath is past, that you would appoint me a set time, and remember me!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If a man dies, shall he live again? All the days of my warfare would I wait, until my release should come.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You would call, and I would answer you. You would have a desire to the work of your hands.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But now you count my steps. Don’t you watch over my sin?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My disobedience is sealed up in a bag. You fasten up my iniquity.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “But the mountain falling comes to nothing. The rock is removed out of its place;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The waters wear the stones. The torrents of it wash away the dust of the earth. So you destroy the hope of man.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You forever prevail against him, and he departs. You change his face, and send him away.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: His sons come to honor, and he doesn’t know it. They are brought low, but he doesn’t perceive it of them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But his flesh on him has pain, and his soul within him mourns.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Should a wise man answer with vain knowledge, and fill himself with the east wind?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Should he reason with unprofitable talk, or with speeches with which he can do no good?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yes, you do away with fear, and hinder devotion before God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For your iniquity teaches your mouth, and you choose the language of the crafty.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Your own mouth condemns you, and not I. Yes, your own lips testify against you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Are you the first man who was born? Or were you brought out before the hills?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Have you heard the secret counsel of God? Do you limit wisdom to yourself?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: What do you know, that we don’t know? What do you understand, which is not in us?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: With us are both the gray-headed and the very aged men, much elder than your father.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Are the consolations of God too small for you, even the word that is gentle toward you?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Why does your heart carry you away? Why do your eyes flash,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: That you turn your spirit against God, and let such words go out of your mouth?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: What is man, that he should be clean? What is he who is born of a woman, that he should be righteous?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, he puts no trust in his holy ones. Yes, the heavens are not clean in his sight;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: how much less one who is abominable and corrupt, a man who drinks iniquity like water!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “I will show you, listen to me; that which I have seen I will declare

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: (which wise men have told by their fathers, and have not hidden it;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: to whom alone the land was given, and no stranger passed among them):

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: the wicked man writhes in pain all his days, even the number of years that are laid up for the oppressor.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A sound of terrors is in his ears. In prosperity the destroyer shall come on him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He doesn’t believe that he shall return out of darkness. He is waited for by the sword.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He wanders abroad for bread, saying, ‘Where is it?’ He knows that the day of darkness is ready at his hand.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Distress and anguish make him afraid. They prevail against him, as a king ready to the battle.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Because he has stretched out his hand against God, and behaves himself proudly against the Almighty;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: he runs at him with a stiff neck, with the thick shields of his bucklers;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: because he has covered his face with his fatness, and gathered fat on his thighs.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He has lived in desolate cities, in houses which no one inhabited, which were ready to become heaps.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He shall not be rich, neither shall his substance continue, neither shall their possessions be extended on the earth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He shall not depart out of darkness. The flame shall dry up his branches. By the breath of God’s mouth shall he go away.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Let him not trust in emptiness, deceiving himself; for emptiness shall be his reward.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It shall be accomplished before his time. His branch shall not be green.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He shall shake off his unripe grape as the vine, and shall cast off his flower as the olive tree.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the company of the godless shall be barren, and fire shall consume the tents of bribery.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They conceive mischief, and produce iniquity. Their heart prepares deceit.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Job answered,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “I have heard many such things. You are all miserable comforters!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Shall vain words have an end? Or what provokes you that you answer?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I also could speak as you do. If your soul were in my soul’s place, I could join words together against you, and shake my head at you,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: but I would strengthen you with my mouth. The solace of my lips would relieve you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Though I speak, my grief is not subsided. Though I forbear, what am I eased?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But now, God, you have surely worn me out. You have made desolate all my company.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You have shriveled me up. This is a witness against me. My leanness rises up against me. It testifies to my face.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He has torn me in his wrath, and persecuted me. He has gnashed on me with his teeth. My adversary sharpens his eyes on me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They have gaped on me with their mouth. They have struck me on the cheek reproachfully. They gather themselves together against me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: God delivers me to the ungodly, and casts me into the hands of the wicked.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I was at ease, and he broke me apart. Yes, he has taken me by the neck, and dashed me to pieces. He has also set me up for his target.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: His archers surround me. He splits my kidneys apart, and does not spare. He pours out my gall on the ground.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He breaks me with breach on breach. He runs on me like a giant.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I have sewed sackcloth on my skin, and have thrust my horn in the dust.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My face is red with weeping. Deep darkness is on my eyelids.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Although there is no violence in my hands, and my prayer is pure.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Earth, don’t cover my blood. Let my cry have no place to rest.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Even now, behold, my witness is in heaven. He who vouches for me is on high.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My friends scoff at me. My eyes pour out tears to God,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: that he would maintain the right of a man with God, of a son of man with his neighbor!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For when a few years have come, I shall go the way of no return.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “My spirit is consumed. My days are extinct, And the grave is ready for me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Surely there are mockers with me. My eye dwells on their provocation.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Now give a pledge, be collateral for me with yourself. Who is there who will strike hands with me?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For you have hidden their heart from understanding, Therefore you shall not exalt them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He who denounces his friends for plunder, Even the eyes of his children shall fail.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “But he has made me a byword of the people. They spit in my face.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My eye also is dim by reason of sorrow. All my members are as a shadow.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Upright men shall be astonished at this. The innocent shall stir up himself against the godless.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yet shall the righteous hold on his way. He who has clean hands shall grow stronger and stronger.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But as for you all, come on now again; I shall not find a wise man among you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My days are past, my plans are broken off, as are the thoughts of my heart.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They change the night into day, saying ‘The light is near’ in the presence of darkness.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If I look for Sheol  as my house, if I have spread my couch in the darkness,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If I have said to corruption, ‘You are my father;’ to the worm, ‘My mother,’ and ‘my sister;’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: where then is my hope? as for my hope, who shall see it?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Shall it go down with me to the gates of Sheol,  or descend together into the dust?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Bildad the Shuhite answered,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “How long will you hunt for words? Consider, and afterwards we will speak.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Why are we counted as animals, which have become unclean in your sight?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You who tear yourself in your anger, shall the earth be forsaken for you? Or shall the rock be removed out of its place?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Yes, the light of the wicked shall be put out, The spark of his fire shall not shine.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The light shall be dark in his tent. His lamp above him shall be put out.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The steps of his strength shall be shortened. His own counsel shall cast him down.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For he is cast into a net by his own feet, and he wanders into its mesh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A snare will take him by the heel. A trap will catch him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A noose is hidden for him in the ground, a trap for him on the path.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Terrors shall make him afraid on every side, and shall chase him at his heels.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: His strength shall be famished. Calamity shall be ready at his side.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The members of his body shall be devoured. The firstborn of death shall devour his members.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He shall be rooted out of the security of his tent. He shall be brought to the king of terrors.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There shall dwell in his tent that which is none of his. Sulfur shall be scattered on his habitation.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: His roots shall be dried up beneath. Above shall his branch be cut off.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: His memory shall perish from the earth. He shall have no name in the street.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He shall be driven from light into darkness, and chased out of the world.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He shall have neither son nor grandson among his people, nor any remaining where he lived.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Those who come after shall be astonished at his day, as those who went before were frightened.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Surely such are the dwellings of the unrighteous. This is the place of him who doesn’t know God.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Job answered,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “How long will you torment me, and crush me with words?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You have reproached me ten times. You aren’t ashamed that you attack me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If it is true that I have erred, my error remains with myself.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If indeed you will magnify yourselves against me, and plead against me my reproach;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: know now that God has subverted me, and has surrounded me with his net.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Behold, I cry out of wrong, but I am not heard. I cry for help, but there is no justice.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He has walled up my way so that I can’t pass, and has set darkness in my paths.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He has stripped me of my glory, and taken the crown from my head.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He has broken me down on every side, and I am gone. My hope he has plucked up like a tree.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He has also kindled his wrath against me. He counts me among his adversaries.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: His troops come on together, build a siege ramp against me, and encamp around my tent.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “He has put my brothers far from me. My acquaintances are wholly estranged from me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My relatives have gone away. My familiar friends have forgotten me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Those who dwell in my house and my maids consider me a stranger. I am an alien in their sight.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I call to my servant, and he gives me no answer. I beg him with my mouth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My breath is offensive to my wife. I am loathsome to the children of my own mother.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Even young children despise me. If I arise, they speak against me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All my familiar friends abhor me. They whom I loved have turned against me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My bones stick to my skin and to my flesh. I have escaped by the skin of my teeth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Have pity on me, have pity on me, you my friends; for the hand of God has touched me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Why do you persecute me as God, and are not satisfied with my flesh?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Oh that my words were now written! Oh that they were inscribed in a book!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: That with an iron pen and lead they were engraved in the rock forever!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But as for me, I know that my Redeemer lives. In the end, he will stand upon the earth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: After my skin is destroyed, then in my flesh shall I see God,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Whom I, even I, shall see on my side. My eyes shall see, and not as a stranger. “My heart is consumed within me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If you say, ‘How we will persecute him!’ because the root of the matter is found in me,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: be afraid of the sword, for wrath brings the punishments of the sword, that you may know there is a judgment.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Zophar the Naamathite answered,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Therefore do my thoughts give answer to me, even by reason of my haste that is in me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I have heard the reproof which puts me to shame. The spirit of my understanding answers me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t you know this from old time, since man was placed on earth,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: that the triumphing of the wicked is short, the joy of the godless but for a moment?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Though his height mount up to the heavens, and his head reach to the clouds,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: yet he shall perish forever like his own dung. Those who have seen him shall say, ‘Where is he?’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He shall fly away as a dream, and shall not be found. Yes, he shall be chased away like a vision of the night.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The eye which saw him shall see him no more, neither shall his place any more see him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: His children shall seek the favor of the poor. His hands shall give back his wealth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: His bones are full of his youth, but youth shall lie down with him in the dust.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Though wickedness is sweet in his mouth, though he hide it under his tongue,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: though he spare it, and will not let it go, but keep it still within his mouth;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: yet his food in his bowels is turned. It is cobra venom within him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He has swallowed down riches, and he shall vomit them up again. God will cast them out of his belly.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He shall suck cobra venom. The viper’s tongue shall kill him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He shall not look at the rivers, the flowing streams of honey and butter.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: That for which he labored he shall restore, and shall not swallow it down. According to the substance that he has gotten, he shall not rejoice.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For he has oppressed and forsaken the poor. He has violently taken away a house, and he shall not build it up.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Because he knew no quietness within him, he shall not save anything of that in which he delights.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There was nothing left that he didn’t devour, therefore his prosperity shall not endure.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In the fullness of his sufficiency, distress shall overtake him. The hand of everyone who is in misery shall come on him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When he is about to fill his belly, God will cast the fierceness of his wrath on him. It will rain on him while he is eating.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He shall flee from the iron weapon. The bronze arrow shall strike him through.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He draws it out, and it comes out of his body. Yes, the glittering point comes out of his liver. Terrors are on him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All darkness is laid up for his treasures. An unfanned fire shall devour him. It shall consume that which is left in his tent.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The heavens shall reveal his iniquity. The earth shall rise up against him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The increase of his house shall depart. They shall rush away in the day of his wrath.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: This is the portion of a wicked man from God, the heritage appointed to him by God.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Job answered,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Listen diligently to my speech. Let this be your consolation.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Allow me, and I also will speak; After I have spoken, mock on.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As for me, is my complaint to man? Why shouldn’t I be impatient?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Look at me, and be astonished. Lay your hand on your mouth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When I remember, I am troubled. Horror takes hold of my flesh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Why do the wicked live, become old, yes, and grow mighty in power?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Their child is established with them in their sight, their offspring before their eyes.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Their houses are safe from fear, neither is the rod of God upon them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Their bulls breed without fail. Their cows calve, and don’t miscarry.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They send out their little ones like a flock. Their children dance.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They sing to the tambourine and harp, and rejoice at the sound of the pipe.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They spend their days in prosperity. In an instant they go down to Sheol.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They tell God, ‘Depart from us, for we don’t want to know about your ways.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: What is the Almighty, that we should serve him? What profit should we have, if we pray to him?’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, their prosperity is not in their hand. The counsel of the wicked is far from me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “How often is it that the lamp of the wicked is put out, that their calamity comes on them, that God distributes sorrows in his anger?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: How often is it that they are as stubble before the wind, as chaff that the storm carries away?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You say, ‘God lays up his iniquity for his children.’ Let him recompense it to himself, that he may know it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Let his own eyes see his destruction. Let him drink of the wrath of the Almighty.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For what does he care for his house after him, when the number of his months is cut off?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Shall any teach God knowledge, since he judges those who are high?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: One dies in his full strength, being wholly at ease and quiet.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: His pails are full of milk. The marrow of his bones is moistened.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Another dies in bitterness of soul, and never tastes of good.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They lie down alike in the dust. The worm covers them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Behold, I know your thoughts, the devices with which you would wrong me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For you say, ‘Where is the house of the prince? Where is the tent in which the wicked lived?’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Haven’t you asked wayfaring men? Don’t you know their evidences,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: that the evil man is reserved to the day of calamity, That they are led out to the day of wrath?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Who shall declare his way to his face? Who shall repay him what he has done?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yet he will be borne to the grave. Men shall keep watch over the tomb.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The clods of the valley shall be sweet to him. All men shall draw after him, as there were innumerable before him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So how can you comfort me with nonsense, because in your answers there remains only falsehood?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Can a man be profitable to God? Surely he who is wise is profitable to himself.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Is it any pleasure to the Almighty, that you are righteous? Or does it benefit him, that you make your ways perfect?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Is it for your piety that he reproves you, that he enters with you into judgment?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Isn’t your wickedness great? Neither is there any end to your iniquities.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For you have taken pledges from your brother for nothing, and stripped the naked of their clothing.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You haven’t given water to the weary to drink, and you have withheld bread from the hungry.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But as for the mighty man, he had the earth. The honorable man, he lived in it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You have sent widows away empty, and the arms of the fatherless have been broken.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore snares are around you. Sudden fear troubles you,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: or darkness, so that you can not see, and floods of waters cover you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Isn’t God in the heights of heaven? See the height of the stars, how high they are!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You say, ‘What does God know? Can he judge through the thick darkness?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Thick clouds are a covering to him, so that he doesn’t see. He walks on the vault of the sky.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Will you keep the old way, which wicked men have trodden,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: who were snatched away before their time, whose foundation was poured out as a stream,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: who said to God, ‘Depart from us;’ and, ‘What can the Almighty do for us?’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yet he filled their houses with good things, but the counsel of the wicked is far from me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The righteous see it, and are glad. The innocent ridicule them,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: saying, ‘Surely those who rose up against us are cut off. The fire has consumed their remnant.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Acquaint yourself with him, now, and be at peace. Thereby good shall come to you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Please receive instruction from his mouth, and lay up his words in your heart.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If you return to the Almighty, you shall be built up, if you put away unrighteousness far from your tents.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Lay your treasure in the dust, the gold of Ophir among the stones of the brooks.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The Almighty will be your treasure, and precious silver to you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For then you will delight yourself in the Almighty, and shall lift up your face to God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall make your prayer to him, and he will hear you. You shall pay your vows.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall also decree a thing, and it shall be established to you. Light shall shine on your ways.

translations.name: World English Bible
