translations.text: When they cast down, you shall say, ‘be lifted up.’ He will save the humble person.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He will even deliver him who is not innocent. Yes, he shall be delivered through the cleanness of your hands.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Job answered,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Even today my complaint is rebellious. His hand is heavy in spite of my groaning.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Oh that I knew where I might find him! That I might come even to his seat!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I would set my cause in order before him, and fill my mouth with arguments.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I would know the words which he would answer me, and understand what he would tell me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Would he contend with me in the greatness of his power? No, but he would listen to me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There the upright might reason with him, so I should be delivered forever from my judge.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “If I go east, he is not there; if west, I can’t find him;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He works to the north, but I can’t see him. He turns south, but I can’t catch a glimpse of him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But he knows the way that I take. When he has tried me, I shall come out like gold.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My foot has held fast to his steps. I have kept his way, and not turned aside.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I haven’t gone back from the commandment of his lips. I have treasured up the words of his mouth more than my necessary food.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But he stands alone, and who can oppose him? What his soul desires, even that he does.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For he performs that which is appointed for me. Many such things are with him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore I am terrified at his presence. When I consider, I am afraid of him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For God has made my heart faint. The Almighty has terrified me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Because I was not cut off before the darkness, neither did he cover the thick darkness from my face.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Why aren’t times laid up by the Almighty? Why don’t those who know him see his days?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There are people who remove the landmarks. They violently take away flocks, and feed them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They drive away the donkey of the fatherless, and they take the widow’s ox for a pledge.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They turn the needy out of the way. The poor of the earth all hide themselves.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, as wild donkeys in the desert, they go out to their work, seeking diligently for food. The wilderness yields them bread for their children.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They cut their food in the field. They glean the vineyard of the wicked.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They lie all night naked without clothing, and have no covering in the cold.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They are wet with the showers of the mountains, and embrace the rock for lack of a shelter.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There are those who pluck the fatherless from the breast, and take a pledge of the poor,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So that they go around naked without clothing. Being hungry, they carry the sheaves.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They make oil within the walls of these men. They tread wine presses, and suffer thirst.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: From out of the populous city, men groan. The soul of the wounded cries out, yet God doesn’t regard the folly.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “These are of those who rebel against the light. They don’t know its ways, nor stay in its paths.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The murderer rises with the light. He kills the poor and needy. In the night he is like a thief.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The eye also of the adulterer waits for the twilight, saying, ‘No eye shall see me.’ He disguises his face.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In the dark they dig through houses. They shut themselves up in the daytime. They don’t know the light.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the morning is to all of them like thick darkness, for they know the terrors of the thick darkness.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “They are foam on the surface of the waters. Their portion is cursed in the earth. They don’t turn into the way of the vineyards.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Drought and heat consume the snow waters, so does Sheol  those who have sinned.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The womb shall forget him. The worm shall feed sweetly on him. He shall be no more remembered. Unrighteousness shall be broken as a tree.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He devours the barren who don’t bear. He shows no kindness to the widow.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yet God preserves the mighty by his power. He rises up who has no assurance of life.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: God gives them security, and they rest in it. His eyes are on their ways.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They are exalted; yet a little while, and they are gone. Yes, they are brought low, they are taken out of the way as all others, and are cut off as the tops of the ears of grain.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If it isn’t so now, who will prove me a liar, and make my speech worth nothing?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Bildad the Shuhite answered,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Dominion and fear are with him. He makes peace in his high places.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Can his armies be counted? On whom does his light not arise?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: How then can man be just with God? Or how can he who is born of a woman be clean?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, even the moon has no brightness, and the stars are not pure in his sight;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: How much less man, who is a worm, the son of man, who is a worm!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Job answered,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “How have you helped him who is without power! How have you saved the arm that has no strength!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: How have you counseled him who has no wisdom, and plentifully declared sound knowledge!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: To whom have you uttered words? Whose spirit came out of you?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “The departed spirits tremble, those beneath the waters and all that live in them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Sheol  is naked before God, and Abaddon  has no covering.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He stretches out the north over empty space, and hangs the earth on nothing.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He binds up the waters in his thick clouds, and the cloud is not burst under them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He encloses the face of his throne, and spreads his cloud on it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He has described a boundary on the surface of the waters, and to the confines of light and darkness.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The pillars of heaven tremble and are astonished at his rebuke.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He stirs up the sea with his power, and by his understanding he strikes through Rahab.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: By his Spirit the heavens are garnished. His hand has pierced the swift serpent.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, these are but the outskirts of his ways. How small a whisper do we hear of him! But the thunder of his power who can understand?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Job again took up his parable, and said,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “As God lives, who has taken away my right, the Almighty, who has made my soul bitter

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: (for the length of my life is still in me, and the spirit of God is in my nostrils);

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: surely my lips shall not speak unrighteousness, neither shall my tongue utter deceit.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Far be it from me that I should justify you. Until I die I will not put away my integrity from me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I hold fast to my righteousness, and will not let it go. My heart shall not reproach me so long as I live.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Let my enemy be as the wicked. Let him who rises up against me be as the unrighteous.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For what is the hope of the godless, when he is cut off, when God takes away his life?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Will God hear his cry when trouble comes on him?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Will he delight himself in the Almighty, and call on God at all times?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will teach you about the hand of God. That which is with the Almighty will I not conceal.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, all of you have seen it yourselves; why then have you become altogether vain?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “This is the portion of a wicked man with God, the heritage of oppressors, which they receive from the Almighty.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If his children are multiplied, it is for the sword. His offspring shall not be satisfied with bread.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Those who remain of him shall be buried in death. His widows shall make no lamentation.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Though he heap up silver as the dust, and prepare clothing as the clay;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: he may prepare it, but the just shall put it on, and the innocent shall divide the silver.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He builds his house as the moth, as a booth which the watchman makes.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He lies down rich, but he shall not do so again. He opens his eyes, and he is not.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Terrors overtake him like waters. A storm steals him away in the night.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The east wind carries him away, and he departs. It sweeps him out of his place.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For it hurls at him, and does not spare, as he flees away from his hand.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Men shall clap their hands at him, and shall hiss him out of his place.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Surely there is a mine for silver, and a place for gold which they refine.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Iron is taken out of the earth, and copper is smelted out of the ore.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Man sets an end to darkness, and searches out, to the furthest bound, the stones of obscurity and of thick darkness.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He breaks open a shaft away from where people live. They are forgotten by the foot. They hang far from men, they swing back and forth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As for the earth, out of it comes bread; Underneath it is turned up as it were by fire.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Sapphires come from its rocks. It has dust of gold.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: That path no bird of prey knows, neither has the falcon’s eye seen it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The proud animals have not trodden it, nor has the fierce lion passed by there.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He puts his hand on the flinty rock, and he overturns the mountains by the roots.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He cuts out channels among the rocks. His eye sees every precious thing.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He binds the streams that they don’t trickle. The thing that is hidden he brings out to light.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “But where shall wisdom be found? Where is the place of understanding?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Man doesn’t know its price; Neither is it found in the land of the living.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The deep says, ‘It isn’t in me.’ The sea says, ‘It isn’t with me.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It can’t be gotten for gold, neither shall silver be weighed for its price.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It can’t be valued with the gold of Ophir, with the precious onyx, or the sapphire.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Gold and glass can’t equal it, neither shall it be exchanged for jewels of fine gold.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: No mention shall be made of coral or of crystal. Yes, the price of wisdom is above rubies.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The topaz of Ethiopia shall not equal it, Neither shall it be valued with pure gold.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Where then does wisdom come from? Where is the place of understanding?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Seeing it is hidden from the eyes of all living, and kept close from the birds of the sky.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Destruction and Death say, ‘We have heard a rumor of it with our ears.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “God understands its way, and he knows its place.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For he looks to the ends of the earth, and sees under the whole sky.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He establishes the force of the wind. Yes, he measures out the waters by measure.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When he made a decree for the rain, and a way for the lightning of the thunder;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: then he saw it, and declared it. He established it, yes, and searched it out.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: To man he said, ‘Behold, the fear of the Lord,  that is wisdom. To depart from evil is understanding.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Job again took up his parable, and said,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Oh that I were as in the months of old, as in the days when God watched over me;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: when his lamp shone on my head, and by his light I walked through darkness,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: as I was in the ripeness of my days, when the friendship of God was in my tent,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: when the Almighty was yet with me, and my children were around me,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: when my steps were washed with butter, and the rock poured out streams of oil for me,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: when I went out to the city gate, when I prepared my seat in the street.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The young men saw me and hid themselves. The aged rose up and stood.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The princes refrained from talking, and laid their hand on their mouth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The voice of the nobles was hushed, and their tongue stuck to the roof of their mouth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For when the ear heard me, then it blessed me; and when the eye saw me, it commended me:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Because I delivered the poor who cried, and the fatherless also, who had no one to help him,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: the blessing of him who was ready to perish came on me, and I caused the widow’s heart to sing for joy.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I put on righteousness, and it clothed me. My justice was as a robe and a diadem.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I was eyes to the blind, and feet to the lame.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I was a father to the needy. The cause of him who I didn’t know, I searched out.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I broke the jaws of the unrighteous, and plucked the prey out of his teeth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then I said, ‘I shall die in my own house, I shall count my days as the sand.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My root is spread out to the waters. The dew lies all night on my branch.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My glory is fresh in me. My bow is renewed in my hand.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Men listened to me, waited, and kept silence for my counsel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: After my words they didn’t speak again. My speech fell on them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They waited for me as for the rain. Their mouths drank as with the spring rain.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I smiled on them when they had no confidence. They didn’t reject the light of my face.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I chose out their way, and sat as chief. I lived as a king in the army, as one who comforts the mourners.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “But now those who are younger than I have me in derision, whose fathers I would have disdained to put with my sheep dogs.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Of what use is the strength of their hands to me, men in whom ripe age has perished?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They are gaunt from lack and famine. They gnaw the dry ground, in the gloom of waste and desolation.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They pluck salt herbs by the bushes. The roots of the broom are their food.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They are driven out from among men. They cry after them as after a thief;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So that they dwell in frightful valleys, and in holes of the earth and of the rocks.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Among the bushes they bray; and under the nettles they are gathered together.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They are children of fools, yes, children of base men. They were flogged out of the land.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Now I have become their song. Yes, I am a byword to them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They abhor me, they stand aloof from me, and don’t hesitate to spit in my face.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For he has untied his cord, and afflicted me; and they have thrown off restraint before me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: On my right hand rise the rabble. They thrust aside my feet, They cast up against me their ways of destruction.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They mar my path, They set forward my calamity, without anyone’s help.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As through a wide breach they come, in the middle of the ruin they roll themselves in.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Terrors have turned on me. They chase my honor as the wind. My welfare has passed away as a cloud.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Now my soul is poured out within me. Days of affliction have taken hold on me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In the night season my bones are pierced in me, and the pains that gnaw me take no rest.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: By great force is my garment disfigured. It binds me about as the collar of my coat.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He has cast me into the mire. I have become like dust and ashes.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I cry to you, and you do not answer me. I stand up, and you gaze at me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You have turned to be cruel to me. With the might of your hand you persecute me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You lift me up to the wind, and drive me with it. You dissolve me in the storm.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For I know that you will bring me to death, To the house appointed for all living.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “However doesn’t one stretch out a hand in his fall? Or in his calamity therefore cry for help?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Didn’t I weep for him who was in trouble? Wasn’t my soul grieved for the needy?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When I looked for good, then evil came; When I waited for light, there came darkness.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My heart is troubled, and doesn’t rest. Days of affliction have come on me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I go mourning without the sun. I stand up in the assembly, and cry for help.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I am a brother to jackals, and a companion to ostriches.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My skin grows black and peels from me. My bones are burned with heat.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore my harp has turned to mourning, and my pipe into the voice of those who weep.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “I made a covenant with my eyes, how then should I look lustfully at a young woman?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For what is the portion from God above, and the heritage from the Almighty on high?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Is it not calamity to the unrighteous, and disaster to the workers of iniquity?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Doesn’t he see my ways, and count all my steps?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “If I have walked with falsehood, and my foot has hurried to deceit

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: (let me be weighed in an even balance, that God may know my integrity);

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: if my step has turned out of the way, if my heart walked after my eyes, if any defilement has stuck to my hands,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: then let me sow, and let another eat. Yes, let the produce of my field be rooted out.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “If my heart has been enticed to a woman, and I have laid wait at my neighbor’s door,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: then let my wife grind for another, and let others sleep with her.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For that would be a heinous crime. Yes, it would be an iniquity to be punished by the judges:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For it is a fire that consumes to destruction, and would root out all my increase.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “If I have despised the cause of my male servant or of my female servant, when they contended with me;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: What then shall I do when God rises up? When he visits, what shall I answer him?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Didn’t he who made me in the womb make him? Didn’t one fashion us in the womb?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “If I have withheld the poor from their desire, or have caused the eyes of the widow to fail,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: or have eaten my morsel alone, and the fatherless has not eaten of it

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: (no, from my youth he grew up with me as with a father, her I have guided from my mother’s womb);

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: if I have seen any perish for want of clothing, or that the needy had no covering;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: if his heart hasn’t blessed me, if he hasn’t been warmed with my sheep’s fleece;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: if I have lifted up my hand against the fatherless, because I saw my help in the gate,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: then let my shoulder fall from the shoulder blade, and my arm be broken from the bone.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For calamity from God is a terror to me. Because his majesty, I can do nothing.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “If I have made gold my hope, and have said to the fine gold, ‘You are my confidence;’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If I have rejoiced because my wealth was great, and because my hand had gotten much;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: if I have seen the sun when it shined, or the moon moving in splendor,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and my heart has been secretly enticed, and my hand threw a kiss from my mouth,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: this also would be an iniquity to be punished by the judges; for I should have denied the God who is above.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “If I have rejoiced at the destruction of him who hated me, or lifted up myself when evil found him

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: (yes, I have not allowed my mouth to sin by asking his life with a curse);

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: if the men of my tent have not said, ‘Who can find one who has not been filled with his meat?’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: (the foreigner has not camped in the street, but I have opened my doors to the traveler);

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: if like Adam I have covered my transgressions, by hiding my iniquity in my heart,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: because I feared the great multitude, and the contempt of families terrified me, so that I kept silence, and didn’t go out of the door —

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: oh that I had one to hear me! Behold, here is my signature! Let the Almighty answer me! Let the accuser write my indictment!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Surely I would carry it on my shoulder; and I would bind it to me as a crown.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I would declare to him the number of my steps. as a prince would I go near to him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If my land cries out against me, and its furrows weep together;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: if I have eaten its fruits without money, or have caused its owners to lose their life,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: let briers grow instead of wheat, and stinkweed instead of barley.”  The words of Job are ended.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So these three men ceased to answer Job, because he was righteous in his own eyes.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then the wrath of Elihu the son of Barachel, the Buzite, of the family of Ram, was kindled against Job. His wrath was kindled because he justified himself rather than God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Also his wrath was kindled against his three friends, because they had found no answer, and yet had condemned Job.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now Elihu had waited to speak to Job, because they were elder than he.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When Elihu saw that there was no answer in the mouth of these three men, his wrath was kindled.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Elihu the son of Barachel the Buzite answered, “I am young, and you are very old; Therefore I held back, and didn’t dare show you my opinion.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I said, ‘Days should speak, and multitude of years should teach wisdom.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But there is a spirit in man, and the breath of the Almighty gives them understanding.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It is not the great who are wise, nor the aged who understand justice.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore I said, ‘Listen to me; I also will show my opinion.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Behold, I waited for your words, and I listened for your reasoning, while you searched out what to say.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yes, I gave you my full attention, but there was no one who convinced Job, or who answered his words, among you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Beware lest you say, ‘We have found wisdom, God may refute him, not man;’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: for he has not directed his words against me; neither will I answer him with your speeches.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “They are amazed. They answer no more. They don’t have a word to say.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Shall I wait, because they don’t speak, because they stand still, and answer no more?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I also will answer my part, and I also will show my opinion.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For I am full of words. The spirit within me constrains me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, my breast is as wine which has no vent; like new wineskins it is ready to burst.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will speak, that I may be refreshed. I will open my lips and answer.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Please don’t let me respect any man’s person, neither will I give flattering titles to any man.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For I don’t know how to give flattering titles; or else my Maker would soon take me away.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “However, Job, please hear my speech, and listen to all my words.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: See now, I have opened my mouth. My tongue has spoken in my mouth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My words shall utter the uprightness of my heart. That which my lips know they shall speak sincerely.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The Spirit of God has made me, and the breath of the Almighty gives me life.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If you can, answer me. Set your words in order before me, and stand up.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, I am toward God even as you are. I am also formed out of the clay.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, my terror shall not make you afraid, neither shall my pressure be heavy on you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Surely you have spoken in my hearing, I have heard the voice of your words, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: ‘I am clean, without disobedience. I am innocent, neither is there iniquity in me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, he finds occasions against me. He counts me for his enemy.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He puts my feet in the stocks. He marks all my paths.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Behold, I will answer you. In this you are not just, for God is greater than man.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Why do you strive against him, because he doesn’t give account of any of his matters?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For God speaks once, yes twice, though man pays no attention.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In a dream, in a vision of the night, when deep sleep falls on men, in slumbering on the bed;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then he opens the ears of men, and seals their instruction,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: That he may withdraw man from his purpose, and hide pride from man.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He keeps back his soul from the pit, and his life from perishing by the sword.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He is chastened also with pain on his bed, with continual strife in his bones;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So that his life abhors bread, and his soul dainty food.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: His flesh is so consumed away, that it can’t be seen. His bones that were not seen stick out.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yes, his soul draws near to the pit, and his life to the destroyers.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “If there is beside him an angel, an interpreter, one among a thousand, to show to man what is right for him;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: then God is gracious to him, and says, ‘Deliver him from going down to the pit, I have found a ransom.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: His flesh shall be fresher than a child’s. He returns to the days of his youth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He prays to God, and he is favorable to him, so that he sees his face with joy. He restores to man his righteousness.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He sings before men, and says, ‘I have sinned, and perverted that which was right, and it didn’t profit me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He has redeemed my soul from going into the pit. My life shall see the light.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Behold, God does all these things, twice, yes three times, with a man,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: to bring back his soul from the pit, that he may be enlightened with the light of the living.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Mark well, Job, and listen to me. Hold your peace, and I will speak.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If you have anything to say, answer me. Speak, for I desire to justify you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If not, listen to me. Hold your peace, and I will teach you wisdom.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moreover Elihu answered,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Hear my words, you wise men. Give ear to me, you who have knowledge.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the ear tries words, as the palate tastes food.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Let us choose for us that which is right. Let us know among ourselves what is good.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For Job has said, ‘I am righteous, God has taken away my right:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Notwithstanding my right I am considered a liar. My wound is incurable, though I am without disobedience.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: What man is like Job, who drinks scorn like water,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Who goes in company with the workers of iniquity, and walks with wicked men?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For he has said, ‘It profits a man nothing that he should delight himself with God.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Therefore listen to me, you men of understanding: far be it from God, that he should do wickedness, from the Almighty, that he should commit iniquity.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the work of a man he will render to him, and cause every man to find according to his ways.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yes surely, God will not do wickedly, neither will the Almighty pervert justice.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Who put him in charge of the earth? or who has appointed him over the whole world?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If he set his heart on himself, If he gathered to himself his spirit and his breath,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: all flesh would perish together, and man would turn again to dust.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “If now you have understanding, hear this. Listen to the voice of my words.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Shall even one who hates justice govern? Will you condemn him who is righteous and mighty? —

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Who says to a king, ‘Vile!’ or to nobles, ‘Wicked!’?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Who doesn’t respect the persons of princes, nor respects the rich more than the poor; for they all are the work of his hands.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In a moment they die, even at midnight. The people are shaken and pass away. The mighty are taken away without a hand.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “For his eyes are on the ways of a man. He sees all his goings.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There is no darkness, nor thick gloom, where the workers of iniquity may hide themselves.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For he doesn’t need to consider a man further, that he should go before God in judgment.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He breaks in pieces mighty men in ways past finding out, and sets others in their place.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore he takes knowledge of their works. He overturns them in the night, so that they are destroyed.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He strikes them as wicked men in the open sight of others;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: because they turned aside from following him, and wouldn’t pay attention to any of his ways,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: so that they caused the cry of the poor to come to him. He heard the cry of the afflicted.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When he gives quietness, who then can condemn? When he hides his face, who then can see him? Alike whether to a nation, or to a man,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: that the godless man may not reign, that there be no one to ensnare the people.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “For has any said to God, ‘I am guilty, but I will not offend any more.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Teach me that which I don’t see. If I have done iniquity, I will do it no more’?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Shall his recompense be as you desire, that you refuse it? For you must choose, and not I. Therefore speak what you know.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Men of understanding will tell me, yes, every wise man who hears me:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: ‘Job speaks without knowledge. His words are without wisdom.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I wish that Job were tried to the end, because of his answering like wicked men.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For he adds rebellion to his sin. He claps his hands among us, and multiplies his words against God.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moreover Elihu answered,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Do you think this to be your right, or do you say, ‘My righteousness is more than God’s,’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: That you ask, ‘What advantage will it be to you? What profit shall I have, more than if I had sinned?’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will answer you, and your companions with you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Look to the heavens, and see. See the skies, which are higher than you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If you have sinned, what effect do you have against him? If your transgressions are multiplied, what do you do to him?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If you are righteous, what do you give him? Or what does he receive from your hand?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Your wickedness may hurt a man as you are, and your righteousness may profit a son of man.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “By reason of the multitude of oppressions they cry out. They cry for help by reason of the arm of the mighty.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But no one says, ‘Where is God my Maker, who gives songs in the night,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: who teaches us more than the animals of the earth, and makes us wiser than the birds of the sky?’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There they cry, but no one gives answer, because of the pride of evil men.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Surely God will not hear an empty cry, neither will the Almighty regard it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: How much less when you say you don’t see him. The cause is before him, and you wait for him!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But now, because he has not visited in his anger, neither does he greatly regard arrogance.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore Job opens his mouth with empty talk, and he multiplies words without knowledge.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Elihu also continued, and said,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Bear with me a little, and I will show you; for I still have something to say on God’s behalf.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will get my knowledge from afar, and will ascribe righteousness to my Maker.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For truly my words are not false. One who is perfect in knowledge is with you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Behold, God is mighty, and doesn’t despise anyone. He is mighty in strength of understanding.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He doesn’t preserve the life of the wicked, but gives to the afflicted their right.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He doesn’t withdraw his eyes from the righteous, but with kings on the throne, he sets them forever, and they are exalted.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If they are bound in fetters, and are taken in the cords of afflictions,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: then he shows them their work, and their transgressions, that they have behaved themselves proudly.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He also opens their ears to instruction, and commands that they return from iniquity.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If they listen and serve him, they shall spend their days in prosperity, and their years in pleasures.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But if they don’t listen, they shall perish by the sword; they shall die without knowledge.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “But those who are godless in heart lay up anger. They don’t cry for help when he binds them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They die in youth. Their life perishes among the unclean.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He delivers the afflicted by their affliction, and opens their ear in oppression.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yes, he would have allured you out of distress, into a wide place, where there is no restriction. That which is set on your table would be full of fatness.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “But you are full of the judgment of the wicked. Judgment and justice take hold of you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t let riches entice you to wrath, neither let the great size of a bribe turn you aside.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Would your wealth sustain you in distress, or all the might of your strength?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t desire the night, when people are cut off in their place.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Take heed, don’t regard iniquity; for you have chosen this rather than affliction.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, God is exalted in his power. Who is a teacher like him?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Who has prescribed his way for him? Or who can say, ‘You have committed unrighteousness?’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Remember that you magnify his work, whereof men have sung.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All men have looked on it. Man sees it afar off.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, God is great, and we don’t know him. The number of his years is unsearchable.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For he draws up the drops of water, which distill in rain from his vapor,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Which the skies pour down and which drop on man abundantly.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yes, can any understand the spreading of the clouds, and the thunderings of his pavilion?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, he spreads his light around him. He covers the bottom of the sea.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For by these he judges the people. He gives food in abundance.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He covers his hands with the lightning, and commands it to strike the mark.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Its noise tells about him, and the livestock also concerning the storm that comes up.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Yes, at this my heart trembles, and is moved out of its place.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Hear, oh, hear the noise of his voice, the sound that goes out of his mouth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He sends it out under the whole sky, and his lightning to the ends of the earth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: After it a voice roars. He thunders with the voice of his majesty. He doesn’t hold back anything when his voice is heard.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: God thunders marvelously with his voice. He does great things, which we can’t comprehend.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For he says to the snow, ‘Fall on the earth;’ likewise to the shower of rain, and to the showers of his mighty rain.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He seals up the hand of every man, that all men whom he has made may know it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then the animals take cover, and remain in their dens.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Out of its room comes the storm, and cold out of the north.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: By the breath of God, ice is given, and the width of the waters is frozen.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yes, he loads the thick cloud with moisture. He spreads abroad the cloud of his lightning.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It is turned around by his guidance, that they may do whatever he commands them on the surface of the habitable world,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Whether it is for correction, or for his land, or for loving kindness, that he causes it to come.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Listen to this, Job. Stand still, and consider the wondrous works of God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Do you know how God controls them, and causes the lightning of his cloud to shine?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Do you know the workings of the clouds, the wondrous works of him who is perfect in knowledge?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You whose clothing is warm, when the earth is still by reason of the south wind?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Can you, with him, spread out the sky, which is strong as a cast metal mirror?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Teach us what we shall tell him, for we can’t make our case by reason of darkness.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Will it be told him that I would speak? Or should a man wish that he were swallowed up?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now men don’t see the light which is bright in the skies, but the wind passes, and clears them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Out of the north comes golden splendor. With God is awesome majesty.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: We can’t reach the Almighty. He is exalted in power. In justice and great righteousness, he will not oppress.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore men revere him. He doesn’t regard any who are wise of heart.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Yahweh answered Job out of the whirlwind,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Who is this who darkens counsel by words without knowledge?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Brace yourself like a man, for I will question you, then you answer me!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Where were you when I laid the foundations of the earth? Declare, if you have understanding.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Who determined its measures, if you know? Or who stretched the line on it?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Whereupon were its foundations fastened? Or who laid its cornerstone,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: when the morning stars sang together, and all the sons of God shouted for joy?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Or who shut up the sea with doors, when it broke out of the womb,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: when I made clouds its garment, and wrapped it in thick darkness,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: marked out for it my bound, set bars and doors,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and said, ‘Here you may come, but no further. Here your proud waves shall be stayed?’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Have you commanded the morning in your days, and caused the dawn to know its place;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: that it might take hold of the ends of the earth, and shake the wicked out of it?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It is changed as clay under the seal, and presented as a garment.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: From the wicked, their light is withheld. The high arm is broken.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Have you entered into the springs of the sea? Or have you walked in the recesses of the deep?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Have the gates of death been revealed to you? Or have you seen the gates of the shadow of death?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Have you comprehended the earth in its width? Declare, if you know it all.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “What is the way to the dwelling of light? As for darkness, where is its place,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: that you should take it to its bound, that you should discern the paths to its house?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Surely you know, for you were born then, and the number of your days is great!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Have you entered the treasuries of the snow, or have you seen the treasures of the hail,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: which I have reserved against the time of trouble, against the day of battle and war?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: By what way is the lightning distributed, or the east wind scattered on the earth?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Who has cut a channel for the flood water, or the path for the thunderstorm;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: To cause it to rain on a land where no man is; on the wilderness, in which there is no man;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: to satisfy the waste and desolate ground, to cause the tender grass to grow?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Does the rain have a father? Or who fathers the drops of dew?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Out of whose womb came the ice? The gray frost of the sky, who has given birth to it?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The waters become hard like stone, when the surface of the deep is frozen.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Can you bind the cluster of the Pleiades, or loosen the cords of Orion?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Can you lead the constellations out in their season? Or can you guide the Bear with her cubs?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Do you know the laws of the heavens? Can you establish its dominion over the earth?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Can you lift up your voice to the clouds, That abundance of waters may cover you?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Can you send out lightnings, that they may go? Do they report to you, ‘Here we are?’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Who has put wisdom in the inward parts? Or who has given understanding to the mind?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Who can count the clouds by wisdom? Or who can pour out the bottles of the sky,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: when the dust runs into a mass, and the clods of earth stick together?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Can you hunt the prey for the lioness, or satisfy the appetite of the young lions,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: when they crouch in their dens, and lie in wait in the thicket?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Who provides for the raven his prey, when his young ones cry to God, and wander for lack of food?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Do you know the time when the mountain goats give birth? Do you watch when the doe bears fawns?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Can you count the months that they fulfill? Or do you know the time when they give birth?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They bow themselves, they bear their young. They end their labor pains.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Their young ones become strong. They grow up in the open field. They go out, and don’t return again.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Who has set the wild donkey free? Or who has loosened the bonds of the swift donkey,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Whose home I have made the wilderness, and the salt land his dwelling place?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He scorns the tumult of the city, neither does he hear the shouting of the driver.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The range of the mountains is his pasture, He searches after every green thing.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Will the wild ox be content to serve you? Or will he stay by your feeding trough?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Can you hold the wild ox in the furrow with his harness? Or will he till the valleys after you?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Will you trust him, because his strength is great? Or will you leave to him your labor?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Will you confide in him, that he will bring home your seed, and gather the grain of your threshing floor?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “The wings of the ostrich wave proudly; but are they the feathers and plumage of love?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For she leaves her eggs on the earth, warms them in the dust,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and forgets that the foot may crush them, or that the wild animal may trample them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: She deals harshly with her young ones, as if they were not hers. Though her labor is in vain, she is without fear,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: because God has deprived her of wisdom, neither has he imparted to her understanding.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When she lifts up herself on high, she scorns the horse and his rider.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Have you given the horse might? Have you clothed his neck with a quivering mane?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Have you made him to leap as a locust? The glory of his snorting is awesome.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He paws in the valley, and rejoices in his strength. He goes out to meet the armed men.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He mocks at fear, and is not dismayed, neither does he turn back from the sword.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The quiver rattles against him, the flashing spear and the javelin.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He eats up the ground with fierceness and rage, neither does he stand still at the sound of the trumpet.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As often as the trumpet sounds he snorts, ‘Aha!’ He smells the battle afar off, the thunder of the captains, and the shouting.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Is it by your wisdom that the hawk soars, and stretches her wings toward the south?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Is it at your command that the eagle mounts up, and makes his nest on high?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: On the cliff he dwells, and makes his home, on the point of the cliff, and the stronghold.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: From there he spies out the prey. His eyes see it afar off.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: His young ones also suck up blood. Where the slain are, there he is.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moreover Yahweh answered Job,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Shall he who argues contend with the Almighty? He who argues with God, let him answer it.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Job answered Yahweh,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Behold, I am of small account. What shall I answer you? I lay my hand on my mouth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I have spoken once, and I will not answer; Yes, twice, but I will proceed no further.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Yahweh answered Job out of the whirlwind,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Now brace yourself like a man. I will question you, and you will answer me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Will you even annul my judgment? Will you condemn me, that you may be justified?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Or do you have an arm like God? Can you thunder with a voice like him?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Now deck yourself with excellency and dignity. Array yourself with honor and majesty.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Pour out the fury of your anger. Look at everyone who is proud, and bring him low.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Look at everyone who is proud, and humble him. Crush the wicked in their place.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Hide them in the dust together. Bind their faces in the hidden place.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then I will also admit to you that your own right hand can save you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “See now, behemoth, which I made as well as you. He eats grass as an ox.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Look now, his strength is in his thighs. His force is in the muscles of his belly.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He moves his tail like a cedar. The sinews of his thighs are knit together.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: His bones are like tubes of brass. His limbs are like bars of iron.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He is the chief of the ways of God. He who made him gives him his sword.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Surely the mountains produce food for him, where all the animals of the field play.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He lies under the lotus trees, in the covert of the reed, and the marsh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The lotuses cover him with their shade. The willows of the brook surround him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, if a river overflows, he doesn’t tremble. He is confident, though the Jordan swells even to his mouth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Shall any take him when he is on the watch, or pierce through his nose with a snare?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Can you draw out Leviathan  with a fish hook, or press down his tongue with a cord?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Can you put a rope into his nose, or pierce his jaw through with a hook?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Will he make many petitions to you, or will he speak soft words to you?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Will he make a covenant with you, that you should take him for a servant forever?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Will you play with him as with a bird? Or will you bind him for your girls?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Will traders barter for him? Will they part him among the merchants?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Can you fill his skin with barbed irons, or his head with fish spears?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Lay your hand on him. Remember the battle, and do so no more.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, the hope of him is in vain. Won’t one be cast down even at the sight of him?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: None is so fierce that he dare stir him up. Who then is he who can stand before me?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Who has first given to me, that I should repay him? Everything under the heavens is mine.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “I will not keep silence concerning his limbs, nor his mighty strength, nor his goodly frame.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Who can strip off his outer garment? Who shall come within his jaws?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Who can open the doors of his face? Around his teeth is terror.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Strong scales are his pride, shut up together with a close seal.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: One is so near to another, that no air can come between them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They are joined to one another. They stick together, so that they can’t be pulled apart.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: His sneezing flashes out light. His eyes are like the eyelids of the morning.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Out of his mouth go burning torches. Sparks of fire leap out.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Out of his nostrils a smoke goes, as of a boiling pot over a fire of reeds.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: His breath kindles coals. A flame goes out of his mouth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There is strength in his neck. Terror dances before him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The flakes of his flesh are joined together. They are firm on him. They can’t be moved.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: His heart is as firm as a stone, yes, firm as the lower millstone.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When he raises himself up, the mighty are afraid. They retreat before his thrashing.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If one attacks him with the sword, it can’t prevail; nor the spear, the dart, nor the pointed shaft.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He counts iron as straw; and brass as rotten wood.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The arrow can’t make him flee. Sling stones are like chaff to him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Clubs are counted as stubble. He laughs at the rushing of the javelin.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: His undersides are like sharp potsherds, leaving a trail in the mud like a threshing sledge.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He makes the deep to boil like a pot. He makes the sea like a pot of ointment.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He makes a path shine after him. One would think the deep had white hair.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: On earth there is not his equal, that is made without fear.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He sees everything that is high. He is king over all the sons of pride.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Job answered Yahweh,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “I know that you can do all things, and that no purpose of yours can be restrained.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You asked, ‘Who is this who hides counsel without knowledge?’ therefore I have uttered that which I did not understand, things too wonderful for me, which I didn’t know.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You said, ‘Listen, now, and I will speak; I will question you, and you will answer me.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I had heard of you by the hearing of the ear, but now my eye sees you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore I abhor myself, and repent in dust and ashes.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It was so, that after Yahweh had spoken these words to Job, Yahweh said to Eliphaz the Temanite, “My wrath is kindled against you, and against your two friends; for you have not spoken of me the thing that is right, as my servant Job has.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now therefore, take to yourselves seven bulls and seven rams, and go to my servant Job, and offer up for yourselves a burnt offering; and my servant Job shall pray for you, for I will accept him, that I not deal with you according to your folly. For you have not spoken of me the thing that is right, as my servant Job has.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So Eliphaz the Temanite and Bildad the Shuhite and Zophar the Naamathite went, and did what Yahweh commanded them, and Yahweh accepted Job.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh turned the captivity of Job, when he prayed for his friends. Yahweh gave Job twice as much as he had before.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then came there to him all his brothers, and all his sisters, and all those who had been of his acquaintance before, and ate bread with him in his house. They comforted him, and consoled him concerning all the evil that Yahweh had brought on him. Everyone also gave him a piece of money,  and everyone a ring of gold.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So Yahweh blessed the latter end of Job more than his beginning. He had fourteen thousand sheep, six thousand camels, one thousand yoke of oxen, and a thousand female donkeys.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He had also seven sons and three daughters.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He called the name of the first, Jemimah; and the name of the second, Keziah; and the name of the third, Keren Happuch.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In all the land were no women found so beautiful as the daughters of Job. Their father gave them an inheritance among their brothers.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: After this Job lived one hundred forty years, and saw his sons, and his sons’ sons, to four generations.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So Job died, being old and full of days.
